hook: ChatGPT keeps giving you 5-step bullet lists when you just need a straight answer. That's not an AI problem. That's a calibration problem.
caption:
ChatGPT keeps giving you 5-step bullet lists when you just need a straight answer. That's not an AI problem. That's a calibration problem.

Here's what's actually happening:

AI defaults to "universally helpful" — which means it writes for the person who needs everything explained, every time. Numbered steps. Bold headers. A summary at the end that repeats everything it just said.

If that's not how your brain works, you're not getting help. You're getting homework.

The fix isn't writing a longer prompt. It's teaching the AI how you think ONCE — and having that travel with you.

Some people want the reasoning before the answer. Some want the answer, full stop, and they'll ask follow-ups if they need more. Some think in systems. Some think in stories. Some need a devil's advocate. Some need validation before critique.

None of these are wrong. All of them get ignored by default AI.

BootFile builds a profile of exactly how your mind works — your reasoning style, your communication preferences, your tolerance for detail — and turns it into an instruction set you drop into any AI conversation.

Same tool. Finally trained on you.

Stop fighting the default. Change it.
